title: AIF & PMS Fund Valuation India | Virtual Auditor | Virtual Auditor
description: NAV computation and fund valuation for AIF, PMS, venture capital funds. SEBI AIF regulations. Quarterly valuation. IBBI Registered Valuer.
bodyText:
HEADING1:AIF & PMS Fund Valuation India | Virtual Auditor

What is AIF & PMS Fund Valuation India | Virtual Auditor | Virtual Auditor?

NAV computation and fund valuation for AIF, PMS, venture capital funds. SEBI AIF regulations. Quarterly valuation. IBBI Registered Valuer.
Virtual Auditor provides professional aif & pms fund valuation india | virtual auditor services across Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai. Our team of qualified Chartered Accountants (FCA), Company Secretaries (ACS), Certified Fraud Examiners (CFE), and IBBI Registered Valuers ensures compliance and accuracy.
HEADING2:Why Choose Virtual Auditor?
• Fellow Chartered Accountant (FCA) with 14+ years experience
• IBBI Registered Valuer (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333)
• Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE)
• Associate Company Secretary (ACS)
• Offices in Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai
• 100+ complex valuations completed
HEADING2:Our Approach
We combine deep regulatory expertise with AI-powered tools to deliver accurate, defensible, and timely results. Every engagement is led by CA V. Viswanathan, ensuring senior-level attention.
HEADING2:Offices
Chennai (HQ): G-131, Ground Floor, Phase 3, Spencer Plaza Mall, Anna Salai, Chennai 600002. Phone: +91 99622 60333.
Bangalore: 7th Floor, Mahalakshmi Chambers, 29, MG Road, Bangalore 560001. Phone: +91 95139 39333.
Mumbai: Workafella, AK Estate, SV Road, Goregaon West, Mumbai 400062. Phone: +91 77000 89597.
Email: support@virtualauditor.in

Virtual Auditor's valuation team is led by CA V. Viswanathan, a SEBI-registered and IBBI-registered valuer (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) with credentials including FCA, ACS, and CFE. Our valuation methodology follows internationally accepted standards including the Income Approach (DCF), Market Approach (comparable transactions), and Asset-Based Approach, depending on the nature and purpose of valuation.

The valuation process typically involves: initial engagement and scope definition, data collection and due diligence, financial analysis and projections, application of appropriate valuation methodologies, draft report review, and final certified valuation report issuance. Turnaround time ranges from 5 to 15 working days depending on complexity.

Our valuation reports are accepted by the Income Tax Department, NCLT, RBI, SEBI, and other regulatory authorities. We serve clients across Chennai, Bangalore, Mumbai, and pan-India through our digital-first engagement model.
